transcript.clean_text: stage six of the volte catalunya and the rain came down it didn't dampen the enthusiasm to be in the breakaway for many however and in fact the opening 20 kilometers were absolutely brutal the full run today 167.6 kilometers out from salut on the coast inland looping back to the neighboring resort of cambrils what was that category one test right at the start of the day that proved to be a launch pad for many in ambition indeed the dashing of many hopes as well with 80 kilometers to go the men out front were of course uae team emirates and this is the reason colombian champion igita was up the road for boro and cara paz from inius grenadiers a team who clearly woke up this morning and said you know what let's do a bardineckia armadia was under pressure he had teammates around him however but those out front what that pair were doing an amazing job well a quality group of 28 included our race leader but for how much longer because the time was holding well if the peloton and indeed those within the pack were starting to lean on uae then it started to look like it was going to be a day when nobody was going to win save for one of the two who were up the road they were working so well together there was speculation that there may well be an arrangement between the pair of them that kara paz would be allowed to take the stage and higita would take the race lead but that would leave a narrow margin between the power of them going into barcelona and basically it was too much to give away so there would be a fight between the pair of them towards x decided to add a little bit of drive and the gap started to tumble down in fact a minute was found in very short order but you know x themselves got no help nobody was giving them a turn certainly not uae who'd done so much work already and so the sights of the caldecetter the last of the categorized climbs on the day taken by the colombian champion igita while the arm was flicking out as you saw for richard carabas to come through and do a turn and he did they shared the duties and in fact it was the the youngster ayuso who was hot on their heels he had no race radio and was unaware that the gap between himself and those behind was quite modest and so that was all going to come together disparate groups were wound in but not the two out front well in the hot pursuit of a group of six they rolled off the big bunch it included quintana who was busy seeing a race that he had once led now slipping away from him and a user was busy trying to fix the radio control and indeed the radio saying to him just wait up we're coming through well there would be a regroup mom but not with the pair up front so we were starting to ask ourselves who would be battling out for third place on the day that's 1500 meters and a little bit of cat and mouse both very fully aware that they didn't want to give away any time at all of the advantage that they built up and it was a handsome margin of well just under the minute with 1300 meters to go as it was igita would be the man to start off the push karapas waiting on his wheel and indeed it looked like aguita had timed this absolutely beautifully with around about 100 meters to go off he set he got himself a gap of more than a bike length extending that and then it started to narrow and then on the throw karapas came through to take it he absolutely delighted that he delivered while the disappointment of akita soon dissipating as he has the race lead well a close margin between the pair of them going into the final day and indeed not such a close margin to those further back dsm rolled the dice with the van der nabler he wound in though and in the end caden groves just dipping his head wondering what might have been well what was was a spectacular drive today carapan's taking it ahead of egita caden groves in third place so lots of broken dreams i think you might say fantastic result for cara pass and india's grenadiers and a new leader in this race igita leads by a margin of just 16 seconds from kara paz like the minute almeida and quintana but it looks like the pair who are in action today will be amongst those that takes the title great day of racing in prospect then in barcelona which one of them will it be well there's bonus seconds straight out of the back door with an early sprint there's a late sprint as well before we get to our circuit monjus climb and we'll be doing that six times as we run to barcelona a fascinating race will then of course finally draw to a close can't wait you
